WorkerProfile.title: WorkerProfile
WorkerProfile.description: Represents the profile of a worker who has joined the Guild, including their personal details, skills, and performance metrics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkerProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the worker.

lastName.description: The last name of the worker.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the worker.

location.description: The geographical location where the worker is available for jobs (e.g., city, region).

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the worker's profile image.

skillIds.description: References to Skills that the worker possesses. (Relationship: WorkerProfile N:N Skill)

categoryIds.description: References to JobCategories the worker prefers to work in. (Relationship: WorkerProfile N:N JobCategory)

experienceYears.description: The number of years of experience the worker has in their field.

status.description: The approval status of the worker's profile.

currentRank.description: The current ranking level of the worker based on performance metrics.

averageRating.description: The average rating received by the worker, derived from completed job ratings.

jobsCompletedCount.description: The total number of jobs successfully completed by the worker.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the worker profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the worker profile was last updated.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Defines a specific skill that a worker can possess, used for categorizing worker capabilities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'Plumbing', 'Electrical Wiring').

description.description: A brief description of the skill.

JobCategory.title: JobCategory
JobCategory.description: Defines a category for jobs, allowing customers to specify the type of service needed and workers to specialize.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the job category (e.g., 'Yard Work', 'House Cleaning').

description.description: A detailed description of the services included in this category.

iconUrl.description: URL to an icon representing the job category for UI display.

JobRequest.title: JobRequest
JobRequest.description: Represents a job request posted by a customer, detailing the service required, budget, and schedule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the JobRequest entity.

customerFirstName.description: The first name of the customer requesting the job.

customerLastName.description: The last name of the customer requesting the job.

customerPhoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the customer.

customerLocation.description: The location where the job needs to be performed.

categoryId.description: Reference to the JobCategory this request belongs to. (Relationship: JobCategory 1:N JobRequest)

description.description: A detailed description of the job requested by the customer.

budgetAmount.description: The budget amount proposed by the customer for the job.

budgetCurrency.description: The currency of the budget amount (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

requestedDateTime.description: The preferred date and time for the job.

postedAt.description: Timestamp when the job request was posted.

status.description: The current status of the job request.

assignedWorkerId.description: Reference to the WorkerProfile assigned to this job. (Relationship: WorkerProfile 1:N JobRequest)

assignedSubGuildId.description: Reference to the SubGuild assigned to this job, if applicable. (Relationship: SubGuild 1:N JobRequest)

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the job was marked as completed.

customerRatingId.description: Reference to the Rating provided by the customer for this job. (Relationship: Rating 1:1 JobRequest)

paymentTransactionId.description: Reference to the Transaction associated with this job's payment. (Relationship: Transaction 1:1 JobRequest)

Rating.title: Rating
Rating.description: Stores individual ratings given by customers to workers after job completion, contributing to worker ranking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Rating entity.

workerId.description: Reference to the WorkerProfile being rated. (Relationship: WorkerProfile 1:N Rating)

jobId.description: Reference to the JobRequest for which the rating was given. (Relationship: JobRequest 1:1 Rating)

score.description: The rating score given (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: Optional text comment or review accompanying the rating.

ratedAt.description: Timestamp when the rating was provided.

SubGuild.title: SubGuild
SubGuild.description: Represents a team or company (Sub-Guild) formed by high-ranked workers to accept and manage jobs collectively.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SubGuild entity.

name.description: The name of the Sub-Guild or team.

leaderWorkerId.description: Reference to the WorkerProfile who is the leader of this Sub-Guild. (Relationship: WorkerProfile 1:N SubGuild)

memberWorkerIds.description: References to WorkerProfiles who are members of this Sub-Guild. (Relationship: WorkerProfile N:N SubGuild)

description.description: A description of the Sub-Guild, its specializations, or services.

averageRating.description: The average rating received by the Sub-Guild, derived from jobs completed by its members.

jobsCompletedCount.description: The total number of jobs successfully completed by the Sub-Guild.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the Sub-Guild was created.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records financial transactions related to job requests, including customer payments, platform fees, and worker payouts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

jobId.description: Reference to the JobRequest associated with this payment transaction. (Relationship: JobRequest 1:1 Transaction)

amountPaidByCustomer.description: The total amount paid by the customer for the job.

platformFeeAmount.description: The amount taken by the platform as a service fee.

workerPayoutAmount.description: The amount paid out to the worker or Sub-Guild after platform fees.

status.description: The current status of the payment transaction.

transactionDate.description: Timestamp when the transaction status was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual worker profiles. 'workerId' must match the authenticated user's UID (request.auth.uid). This collection supports both self-management by the worker (read/update their own profile) and public viewing of approved profiles (read by anyone if status is 'APPROVED'). Administrators have full access. No additional denormalization for authorization is needed as 'workerId' is the document ID, enabling direct path-based security rules.

Stores predefined skills available for workers. This is a public collection, allowing read access for all users to discover skills. Create, update, and delete access are restricted to administrators. This supports scalable category management as per app requirements, ensuring QAPs for listing are straightforward as all documents share the same public read security posture.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for a skill.

definition.description: Stores predefined job categories. This is a public collection, allowing read access for all users (customers and workers) to browse categories. Create, update, and delete access are restricted to administrators. It ensures scalable category management and supports QAPs for listing as all documents share the same public read security posture.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a job category.

definition.description: Stores customer job requests. To achieve Authorization Independence, it includes denormalized 'customerId' (the UID of the customer who posted the job). If assigned to a SubGuild, it also includes 'assignedSubGuildLeaderId' and 'assignedSubGuildMembers' (a map `{uid: boolean}` indicating all SubGuild members with access), denormalized from the SubGuild document. This allows customers to manage their own requests, workers to see open jobs (QAP via 'status' field), and assigned workers/SubGuild members/leaders to access their relevant jobs directly without 'get()' operations to other collections. Administrators have full access.

params.name: jobRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for a job request.

definition.description: Stores customer ratings for workers. To ensure Authorization Independence, it includes denormalized 'customerId' (the UID of the customer who gave the rating) and 'workerId' (the UID of the worker who received the rating). This allows customers to manage (create, read, update, delete) their own given ratings and workers to view (read) their received ratings efficiently without needing to fetch the parent JobRequest document via 'get()'. Administrators have full access.

params.name: ratingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a rating.

definition.description: Stores information about worker Sub-Guilds. To maintain Authorization Independence and support collaborative access, it includes a denormalized 'members' map (`{workerId: 'role'}`) containing all member UIDs and their roles (e.g., 'leader', 'member'). The 'leaderWorkerId' field is also present and consistent with the 'members' map. This allows the leader and members to access the SubGuild data directly without relying on 'get()' calls to other collections. Public aspects of SubGuilds are readable by all. Administrators have full access.

params.name: subGuildId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Sub-Guild.

definition.description: Records financial transactions related to job requests. To ensure Authorization Independence, it includes denormalized 'customerId' and 'workerId' (copied from the associated JobRequest). If the job was assigned to a SubGuild, it also includes 'subGuildLeaderId' and 'subGuildMembers' (map) copied from the JobRequest. This allows customers, workers, and SubGuild members to access their relevant transactions directly and securely without cross-document 'get()' calls, enabling efficient QAPs for transaction history.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a transaction.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for storing administrator roles. Authorization (DBAC) is based on the existence of a document where 'adminId' matches 'request.auth.uid'. This provides explicit and easily verifiable global role management, adhering to Authorization Independence as it requires no 'get()' calls to other collections. The schema assumes a minimal document structure, such as `{ "role": "admin" }`, where the document ID is the 'userId'.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Workers Guild is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Query as Authorization Patterns (QAPs) to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability.

**1. Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
This core principle is achieved by denormalizing crucial authorization context directly into the documents where it's needed, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For example:
*   **JobRequest:** Each `JobRequest` document explicitly includes `customerId` (the ID of the customer who posted the job). If a job is assigned to a `SubGuild`, it further includes `assignedSubGuildLeaderId` and `assignedSubGuildMembers` (a map of member UIDs to boolean `true`) directly in the `JobRequest` document. This allows customers, assigned workers, and SubGuild members/leaders to access their relevant jobs without rules needing to fetch parent `SubGuild` or `User` documents.
*   **Rating:** `Rating` documents include both `customerId` (who gave the rating) and `workerId` (who received it), enabling both customers and workers to access their respective ratings directly.
*   **SubGuild:** The `SubGuild` document itself contains a `members` map (`{ workerId: 'role' }`), allowing security rules to instantly check if `request.auth.uid` is a leader or member without an additional `get()` operation.
*   **Transaction:** `Transaction` documents denormalize `customerId`, `workerId`, and if applicable, `subGuildLeaderId` and `subGuildMembers` from the associated `JobRequest`. This ensures relevant parties can access their payment history securely and efficiently.
*   **WorkerProfile:** Worker profiles are stored under `/worker_profiles/{workerId}`, where `{workerId}` is the `request.auth.uid`, making ownership implicit and authorization direct.
*   **AdminRole:** A dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminId}` uses existence-based authorization (DBAC). An admin's UID as a document ID in this collection grants admin privileges, verified without any complex lookups.

**2. Structural Segregation for Homogeneous Security Posture:**
Collections are designed so that all documents within them share similar security requirements, simplifying rules:
*   `/skills` and `/job_categories` are globally readable by all, with write access restricted to administrators.
*   `/worker_profiles` documents are primarily owned by the individual worker (`request.auth.uid == workerId`), but public (read-only) access is granted to approved profiles, and full access to administrators.
*   `/job_requests`, `/ratings`, `/sub_guilds`, and `/transactions` are accessible based on explicit denormalized ownership/membership fields within each document, avoiding mixed access patterns within a single collection that would complicate rules.

**3. Query as Authorization Patterns (QAPs):**
The structure supports efficient and secure `list` operations without relying on security rules acting as filters (which is an anti-pattern).
*   Customers can list their own jobs using `collection('job_requests').where('customerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. The rule then simply verifies `resource.data.customerId == request.auth.uid`.
*   Workers can list available open jobs with `collection('job_requests').where('status', '==', 'OPEN')`. The rules permit reading documents with `status == 'OPEN'`, with further filtering by category/location expected on the client-side.
*   Workers can list their assigned jobs (`.where('assignedWorkerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`) or ratings received (`.where('workerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`).
*   SubGuild members can list their SubGuilds (`.where('members.request.auth.uid', '!=', null)`) or assigned jobs (`.where('assignedSubGuildMembers.request.auth.uid', '==', true)`).

This design minimizes rule complexity, prevents security vulnerabilities arising from `get()` dependencies, and provides a clear, maintainable authorization model for the Workers Guild platform.
